steps.title: Use circle standard form
steps.content:
A circle with center $(h,k)$ and radius $r$ has equation
$$(x-h)^2+(y-k)^2=r^2$$

steps.title: Substitute the center
steps.content:
The center is $(11,17)$, so the left side is
$$(x-11)^2+(y-17)^2$$

steps.title: Square the radius
steps.content:
The radius is $6k$, so the right side must be
$$(6k)^2=36k^2$$
Thus the equation is
$$(x-11)^2+(y-17)^2=36k^2$$
The answer is B.
